purpose: Reusable constraints for the ProteinMPNN stage after RFdiffusion backbone generation.

global_rules.objective: Retain or enrich correctly positioned solvent-exposed positive patches while preserving fold, linker geometry, disulfides, solubility, and diversity.
do_not: Do not maximize total net positive charge globally.
Do not assume that every Lys/Arg substitution improves sweetness.
Do not mutate buried residues solely to change charge.
Do not treat predicted receptor binding as receptor activation or sweetness.
required_downstream_checks: Solvent accessibility and electrostatic surface calculation
Approved sensory testing only after safety and ethics review

monellin.reference_construct: MNEI: natural chain B + Gly-Phe linker + natural chain A
design_notes: Y65R and Q28K are literature-supported examples of position-specific positive-charge engineering.
M42R, Y63R, and D68R illustrate that adding a positive charge at the wrong position can reduce sweetness.
C41S and E23Q are literature precedents to evaluate separately for aggregation and stability; they are not automatic defaults.
Preserve all eight modeled cysteines during the first ProteinMPNN round.
Treat surface charge as a spatial pattern and retain multiple sequence families for later receptor-function screening.

primary_references.title: Solution structure of a sweet protein: NMR study of MNEI, a single chain monellin
primary_references.relevance: MNEI is a 96-residue construct linking natural monellin chains B and A with a Gly-Phe dipeptide.

primary_references.title: Sweeter and stronger: enhancing sweetness and stability of the single chain monellin MNEI through molecular design
primary_references.relevance: Shows that charge placement, not global positive charge alone, is important; reports Y65R, Q28K and counterexamples.

primary_references.title: RFdiffusion official usage documentation
primary_references.relevance: Defines contig motif/linker syntax, partial-diffusion length constraints, and conservative noise guidance.
